does: This paper proposes Ellipsoid Control, a white-list jailbreak defense that protects LLMs by directly modeling and preserving benign latent representations rather than learning to detect harmful inputs. The method uses representation engineering to constrain model activations to an ellipsoidal region in latent space that corresponds to benign behavior, ensuring harmless inputs remain unaffected while triggering refusals on arbitrary test inputs including novel jailbreaks. The approach leverages abundant benign data rather than scarce harmful examples, and empirically demonstrates stronger robustness against both known and unseen jailbreak attacks compared to prior black-list representation engineering defenses that rely on harmful supervision.
connects: This work builds on recent representation engineering (RepE) defenses that intervene on LLM latent activations to improve safety, extending a line of research exploring how internal model representations can be manipulated for alignment. It directly responds to limitations of existing black-list approaches that learn jailbreak-specific transformations from collected harmful data, a paradigm inherited from adversarial robustness and machine unlearning. The paper connects to broader work on benign-region preservation and specification gaming in defenses, drawing inspiration from robustness approaches that characterize safe behavior positively rather than enumerating threats. It also relates to data-driven safety research showing how abundant unlabeled benign data can substitute for expensive adversarial annotations.
enables: This white-list perspective opens new directions for scalable LLM defenses that reduce dependence on costly adversarial data collection and curation. The ellipsoidal benign-region framework could inspire more sophisticated geometric approaches to latent space safety, such as learning adaptive or hierarchical regions for different types of benign behavior. The work enables future research into understanding what geometric properties of benign representations confer robustness, and whether such regions generalize across model architectures and scales. It points toward defense mechanisms that guarantee formal properties about the latent region, potential integration with other safety techniques like RLHF or mechanistic interpretability, and applications to other domains where positive specification is more tractable than threat enumeration.
